title: The Official Star Wars Fact File
content:
{{Top|rwm|leg|ref|italics=1}}
{{Youmay|the publication that ran from [[2002]] to [[2005]]|[[The Official Star Wars Fact File (2013)|the 2013 remake]] or [[The Official Star Wars Fact File (2014)|the 2014 one]]}}
{{MultipleIssues|cleanup|citation}}
{{MagazineSeries
|image=[[File:Fact_file_1.jpg]]
|title=''The Official Star Wars Fact File''
|publisher=[[De Agostini]]
|start date=[[December 27]], [[2001]]<ref name="First">{{SW|url=news/the-official-star-wars-fact-files-return-to-our-galaxy|text=''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' Returns to Our Galaxy|nolive=1}}</ref>
|end date=[[September 1]], [[2004]]<ref name="Last">{{FFdates|140}}</ref>
|genre=
|issues=140
}}
'''''The Official Star Wars Fact File''''' was a series of magazines published weekly by [[De Agostini]] from [[2002]] to [[2005]], consisting of 140 issues in total. It was an archive of documents collated from all aspects of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' saga, including information from the films released at the time of its publication, various novels, [[Star Wars comic books|comics]], [[Star Wars video games|video game]]s, and the ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|New Jedi Order]]'' novel series. It also provided some all-new illustrations.

It was published in Spain, Italy (75 issues only), the United Kingdom, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, the Netherlands, Belgium, Poland (4 issues only), Germany (120 issues + index), France (as ''Star Wars "Les Dossiers Officiels"''), Malta, and Mexico. It was later published in Argentina, but only the first issue reached stores.

In Japan, the magazine had additional articles (produced by writers in the UK and US) but a shorter run. The extra articles were added to the UK and Dutch edition of the magazine, extending the run at that point from 120 issues to the final 140. An index containing a list of all articles was also published.

The original concept work was conducted by [[Marcus Hearn]], former editor of [[Titan Magazines|Titan's]] ''[[Star Wars: The Official Magazine]]'', and Andrew Kemp. Many of the writers on the project had also been involved with Titan's licensed magazine. The Fact File was put together for De Agostini by Warrender Grant, based in London.

==Categories {{SectionFlag}}==
The series was organized into six major categories:
*Battles & Events
*Characters
*Planets & Locations
*Droids
*Weapons & Technology
*Vehicles
